title: On the (703,325,150) Cyclic Difference-Set Problem
lean_gate_reason: Lean would only be later archival polish. The present blocker is source-level mathematics: the packet still does not justify promoting the survey row's MC-prime entry into a proven usable 5-multiplier for the exact cyclic case (703,325,150).
micro_paper_assessment: The underlying target still has real micro-paper leverage: one clean exact solve would mostly be the paper. But the current packet is not close to a note, because its title theorem depends on an unsourced promotion of the survey's MC-prime entry to a proven multiplier. Move it aside unless that premise can be repaired quickly and locally.
family_affinity: Strong cyclic multiplier-contraction affinity; the packet lives inside the Gordon-Schmidt / Baumert-Gordon residual-row toolkit.
generalization_signal: The surviving reusable idea is a conditional square obstruction: a verified multiplier that is transitive on the nonzero residues modulo a prime divisor q of v forces k-lambda to be a square.
strongest_honest_claim: The current artifact does not settle the exact cyclic row. What it preserves is a conditional obstruction: if 5 is an actual multiplier for (703,325,150), then the mod-37 contraction forces a square contradiction and no such cyclic difference set can exist.
paper_title_hint: No stable exact-row title is honest yet; the intended exact title becomes honest only if the 5-multiplier premise is sourced or replaced.
candidate_theorem_slice: Conditional slice: if a cyclic difference set admits a multiplier whose image is transitive on the nonzero residues modulo a prime divisor q of v, then k-lambda must be a square; the intended application here is q=37 and t=5.
